en_title: Tim Sweeney Interview: Epic Games CEO on AI in UE6 and Why Games Struggle to Find Success
cn_title: 《Epic Games》Epic CEO谈UE6 AI集成与游戏成功困境
subtitle: 看点来了
opus_summary: Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney与EVP Marcus Wassmer在接受IGN采访时，透露Unreal Engine 6将于2027年底进入抢先体验。

paragraphs.en: A few hours after the State of Unreal keynote speech, founder and CEO of Epic Games Tim Sweeney, alongside EVP of development Marcus Wassmer, met with us to talk about everything new with Unreal Engine. They’d just announced that UE6 is set for early access at the end of 2027 with a 12-14 month period before its full launch, had developers on stage speak to how Unreal made their games technically possible, and, well, showcased AI tools being integrated in future iterations of the engine.
paragraphs.cn: 在State of Unreal主题演讲结束几小时后，Epic Games创始人兼CEO Tim Sweeney与开发执行副总裁Marcus Wassmer与我们见面，聊了聊Unreal Engine的一切新动向。他们刚刚宣布UE6将于2027年底开启抢先体验，并经历12至14个月后正式发布，还邀请了开发者上台讲述Unreal引擎如何让他们的游戏在技术上成为可能，当然，也展示了未来引擎版本中即将集成的AI工具。

paragraphs.en: It feels redundant to say at this point that the games industry is in unprecedented times, but it really is – at the convergence of unaffordable consumer hardware, development studios getting shuttered, and AI getting pushed onto everything in between; this is where we are. With Unreal Engine being so ubiquitous as the framework for which some of the biggest games are made, Epic and its tech has a major role in stewarding the games industry, as fallible as they may be. We talked about all that in the following interview, which you can read or watch below.
paragraphs.cn: 如今再说游戏行业身处前所未有的时代可能有些老套，但事实确实如此——买不起的消费者硬件、不断关停的开发工作室，以及被强加到一切事物中的AI，这几股力量汇聚在一起，这便是我们当下的处境。鉴于Unreal Engine作为许多最大规模游戏的开发框架无处不在，Epic及其技术在管理游戏行业方面扮演着重要角色，尽管他们也难免有失误。我们在以下采访中聊到了所有这些话题，你可以阅读或观看下文。

paragraphs.en: IGN: How is UE6 going to change AAA game development? As it gets more expensive and tools get more advanced, it seems like the idea is to help create an environment that’s more seamless for developers and to cut down on development time. Can you dig a little more into that as you transition over to UE6 in the coming years?
paragraphs.cn: IGN：UE6将如何改变3A游戏开发？随着开发成本越来越高、工具越来越先进，听起来UE6的理念是帮助创造一个对开发者来说更无缝的环境，并缩短开发时间。在接下来过渡到UE6的这几年里，你们能再深入谈谈这一点吗？

paragraphs.en: Wassmer : I think it's two things – one, the efficiency of development. Games are getting bigger, games are getting more difficult [to make]. So, all the architectural work we're doing on the engine should just smooth things out. Not to mention AI creation flows. We showed some demos, and it really should take a lot of the tedium out of game creation and let people just actually focus on the creative part, which should be really nice. Then, the really cool thing, I think we're kind of inventing a new type of economy here, hopefully with the interconnected and interoperable assets between games, which I don’t think has ever been done. I haven't seen it done at scale, at least anyway.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer：我认为主要两点——其一，开发效率。游戏越来越庞大，制作也越来越难。所以我们在引擎架构上所做的所有工作，就是要理顺这些流程。更别提AI创作流程了。我们展示了一些演示，它确实能大幅消除游戏创作中的繁琐工作，让人们真正专注于创意部分，这应该会很棒。然后，真正酷的是，我们或许正在这里发明一种全新的经济形态，希望实现游戏间资产的互联互通与互操作，我觉得这前所未有。至少我从未见过如此大规模的应用。

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : I'm really looking forward to the increased simplicity. I wrote the first generation Unreal Engine and with every generation, out of necessity of taking advantage of new features of hardware and expanding the engine capabilities, we've made the engine progressively more and more complicated, to the point where it's somewhat daunting to open up Unreal Engine 5 and look at the five-level MUI nested menus in some cases.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney：我尤为期待简化的提升。我编写了第一代Unreal Engine，此后每一代，出于利用硬件新功能和扩展引擎能力的必要，我们都让引擎变得越来越复杂，以至于如今打开Unreal Engine 5，有时看到五层深的MUI嵌套菜单，会让人有点望而生畏。

paragraphs.en: The C++ programming model really requires expert level programming, and the great cleanup in Unreal Engine 6 is to adopt the Verse scripting language that we've been pioneering in Fortnite with the Fortnite creator community and bring that into UE6 as the primary way to develop gameplay code. So, it brings the ease of development and programming that you have in an engine like Unity or Godot together with the full power of UE6 on the high-end AAA feature-sets. And to tame complexity of the engine, the ability to prompt a lot of different systems, and have it help you create a particle system and tweak it; it’ll enable you to focus on the details that really matter rather than the boilerplate of creating and repeating the same rote actions over and over. It's going to be really freeing.
paragraphs.cn: C++编程模型确实需要专家级编程水平，而Unreal Engine 6的重大精简就是采用我们在《堡垒之夜》中与创作者社区一起开创的Verse脚本语言，并将其引入UE6，作为开发游戏逻辑代码的主要方式。这样一来，它既带来了类似Unity或Godot等引擎的开发与编程便捷性，又结合了UE6在高端3A功能集上的全部威力。为了驯服引擎的复杂性，能够向各种系统发出提示，并让它帮你创建粒子系统并进行微调，这将让你能够专注于真正重要的细节，而不是重复那些创建和循环的样板化操作。这将是非常解放的事情。

paragraphs.en: I think it will bring back some of the magic that we had in earlier days that each engine had in its first few years before each engine grew more complex. It’s just making it really easy to create stuff, giving the user an immense amount of creative freedom and the feeling that they can exercise it pretty easily without going off and spending hours or days on tutorials.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney 说道：「我认为它将带回早期的魔力，就是每款引擎在其最初几年、在变得复杂之前所拥有的那种魔力。其实就是让创作变得非常简单，给予用户极大的创作自由，并让他们感觉能够轻松施展这种自由，而不必花几个小时或几天去看教程。」

paragraphs.en: IGN: While the State of Unreal keynote was more developer-focused, what would you say to someone on the outside looking in who’s wondering how this affects the games they play?
paragraphs.cn: IGN：「虽然 State of Unreal 主题演讲更面向开发者，但对于那些想了解这对自己玩的游戏会有何影响的外部人士，你有什么想说的？」

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : The biggest opportunity is for games to connect their economies. Not every game wants to connect every part of it; in some cases it's logical and in some cases it’s not. But if you look broadly across games, there's an awful lot of stuff that could be shared in common. Every game with human [characters] could share emotes, and a lot of games with similar art styles could share characters such that from a player's point of view, it would be a whole lot more valuable if everything we bought that is cosmetic in nature worked across all of the different games that we owned.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney 说道：「最大的机遇在于让游戏之间互通经济。并不是每款游戏都想把所有部分都互通；在某些情况下合乎逻辑，在某些情况下则不然。但如果放眼所有游戏，有大量内容可以共享。凡是包含人类角色的游戏都可以共享表情动作，而许多美术风格相似的游戏可以共享角色。这样一来，从玩家角度看，如果我们购买的所有装饰性物品都能在拥有的不同游戏中使用，其价值会大得多。」

paragraphs.en: I think that creates a huge opportunity in the future for an ecosystem where there's lasting value created in the sense that whether or not you're playing the current game you're in, weeks, months, or years from now, the things that you've done and earned in it are of lasting utility to you as long as you're playing some game somewhere.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney 继续说道：「我认为这为未来创造了一个巨大的机遇，构筑一个能够产生持久价值的生态系统。也就是说，不论你当前玩的是哪款游戏，在几周、几个月甚至几年后，你在游戏中做过和获得的东西对你依然有持续的效用，只要你还在玩某个游戏。」

paragraphs.en: Wassmer : On top of that, and just even more directly, I think we're going to continue to see small teams being able to punch above their weight. You take a look at Claire Obscur: Expedition 33 and No Law getting shown off – really small teams doing really, really impressive stuff. I think you're only going to see that trend accelerate as we go toward the UE6 era.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer 补充道：「除此之外，而且更直接的是，我认为我们会继续看到小型团队能够以小博大。看看展示出来的《Claire Obscur: Expedition 33》和《No Law》——非常小的团队做出了非常非常令人印象深刻的作品。我认为随着我们进入 UE6 时代，这一趋势只会加速。」

paragraphs.en: IGN: One of the things that is also top of mind, especially for games built on Unreal, is performance and scaling for mid-level to low-end hardware. UE5 has had its ups and downs when it comes to playing well on handhelds, which have blown up with the Steam Deck and ROG Ally – and people are playing more PC games on lower-end specs. How is that being accounted for with UE6?
paragraphs.cn: IGN：「尤其是对于基于 Unreal 构建的游戏，还有一个备受关注的问题是针对中低端硬件的性能和扩展性。UE5 在掌机上流畅运行方面起起伏伏，而掌机已随 Steam Deck 和 ROG Ally 大爆发——人们正用较低配置玩更多 PC 游戏。UE6 将如何应对这一点？」

paragraphs.en: Wassmer : I think we've actually been addressing it through 5.7 and 5.8 even, and I think the trend will continue through UE6. We're a game developer, too. Fortnite players have the same issues as anyone else and we try to scale from mobile all the way into high-end PC.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer：「我认为我们其实已经通过5.7和5.8在着手解决这个问题了，而且这一趋势会延续到Unreal Engine 6。我们自己也是游戏开发者。《堡垒之夜》的玩家和所有其他玩家一样面临着同样的问题，我们也在努力覆盖从移动端到高端PC的全平台。」

paragraphs.en: You see initiatives like Lumen light we showed on stage as an effort to bring the costs of Lumen down to be accessible to more people for global dynamic illumination. We were showing off Mesh Terrain where you can see the new features we're building that’s built inherently to scale from Nanite to non-Nanite platforms. So, when it cooks down for a non-Nanite platform, it just cooks down to regular meshes that should run efficiently and more efficiently than the old landscape system in most cases.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer：「比如我们在台上展示的Lumen轻量化方案，就是为了降低Lumen的成本，让更多玩家能用上全局动态光照。我们还展示了Mesh Terrain，这是我们在建的新功能，设计上就自带从Nanite到非Nanite平台的缩放能力。所以当它针对非Nanite平台烘焙时，就会变成常规网格，在大多数情况下运行效率比旧的地形系统更高。」

paragraphs.en: So, it's really on our mind. I think you can see games lag a little bit in shipping on engine releases, but as more games kind of hit that 5.7 and 5.8 mark, I think you’ll already see a trend toward efficiency. We have a ton of stuff in the pipeline as we progress towards UE6, and that's going to land in the engine as well.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer：「所以这方面我们一直放在心上。我觉得大家能看到，游戏在采用新版引擎方面总会慢半拍，但随着越来越多的游戏开始用上5.7和5.8，我认为已经能看出效率提升的趋势了。我们在向Unreal Engine 6推进的过程中还储备了大量技术，这些最终也会集成到引擎里。」

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : It's the cumulative set of optimizations that have been done. We have Fortnite back on mobile, we're putting a lot of effort into optimizing the engine so that a developer can ship a game and run on everything from the highest end hardware all the way down to low-end Android and several-year-old iPhones. Over time, we've also come to an increasing appreciation of the need to make the engine automatically scale much more of the content in the game. It's always been the case that with enough effort and trickery, a developer could make their game run on low-end devices. But the more our systems like Nanite can automatically scale, the better. That's going to be a source of ongoing attention.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney：「这是累积优化后的结果。我们把《堡垒之夜》带回了移动平台，我们正在投入大量精力优化引擎，让开发者能做出从最高端硬件一路跑到低端安卓和好几年前的iPhone上的游戏。随着时间的推移，我们也越来越认识到，需要让引擎能自动缩放游戏中更多的内容。以前，只要有足够的努力和巧思，开发者总能设法让游戏在低端设备上运行。但像Nanite这样的系统能自动缩放的内容越多，效果就越好。这将是我们会持续关注的方向。」

paragraphs.en: IGN: As people who have worked with bleeding edge tech for many years, I want to get your perspective on the increasing cost of hardware – even mid-level hardware just to engage with games that are built on these pretty sophisticated technologies. Does it cross your mind when working on new versions or builds of the engine, do you account for that? And what's your perspective on the unprecedented times with pricing and hardware availability?
paragraphs.cn: IGN：你们二位都是跟尖端技术打了多年交道的人，我想听听你们对硬件成本越来越高的看法——如今就连玩一些基于相当复杂技术打造的游戏，都需要中端硬件。你们在开发新版引擎或构建新功能时，会考虑这一点吗？对于目前这种定价和硬件供应都前所未有的时期，你们怎么看？

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : Well, it's an unfortunate and completely unexpected event that AI would surge so much and place so much competitive pressure on memory prices and everything to the point where it's significantly affecting the cost of gaming hardware. I think it's a temporary effect, but temporary over the next two or three years.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney：「嗯，AI突然爆发，给内存价格等各方面带来了巨大的竞争压力，以至于严重影响了游戏硬件的成本，这确实很不幸，也完全出乎意料。我认为这只是暂时现象，但会持续两三年。」

paragraphs.en: I'm sure throughout Asia, there are massive fabs being built to manufacture memory at scales that will eventually relieve the supply pressure. In the meantime, we're going to have to be judicious and spend more time optimizing knowing that we can count less on Moore's Law to solve our problems for us, as the game industry has always done with or without intention.
paragraphs.cn: 我相信在整个亚洲，正在建造大规模的晶圆厂来生产内存，其规模最终将缓解供应压力。与此同时，我们必须审慎行事，花更多时间进行优化，因为我们知道不能像过去那样依赖摩尔定律为我们解决问题——游戏产业无论有意无意，一直是这样做的。

paragraphs.en: Wassmer : We definitely talk about it and think about it as we're doing development, for sure. As Tim says, you can’t count on hardware trends to help us out at the moment. So, it is increasing the pressure for a push on optimization across mobile and mid-spec, for sure.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer：「我们在开发过程中肯定会谈论并思考这个问题。正如Tim所说，目前不能指望硬件趋势来帮忙。所以，这确实加大了对移动端和中端规格优化的压力。」

paragraphs.en: IGN: It’s interesting to see UE5.8 and UE6 using the MCP server and including support for multiple LLMs into its ecosystem. With this increasing ubiquitousness of AI, what's the role of AI in the game development process from your point of view?
paragraphs.cn: IGN：看到虚幻引擎5.8和虚幻引擎6使用MCP服务器，并在生态中纳入对多款大语言模型的支持，这很有意思。随着AI日益普及，从你们的角度看，AI在游戏开发流程中扮演什么角色？

paragraphs.en: Wassmer : I think its role is as a helper where it's useful, right? You go look back at Codegen tools back in last November, they kind of weren't that great. Now they're pretty good, so you can pretty easily put them into engineering pipelines. I think the main thing is you want to make sure of is to use AI to reduce all of the tedium. All the tedious tasks, like, you don't need an engineer to go and spend half a day doing root cause analysis on a crash if you can have a thing do that for you in 20 minutes and then tell them what's going on so they can spend that time optimizing the engine instead, helping a content creator, or whatever. I think it's horses for courses and there will be places where it's not useful, there'll be places where it will.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer：「我认为它的角色是在有用的地方做个帮手，对吧？你回想一下去年11月的代码生成工具，当时还不怎么样。现在已经相当不错了，可以很轻松地把它们整合到工程流水线里。我觉得关键是要确保用AI来减少所有枯燥的工作。所有繁琐的任务，比如不需要工程师花半天时间做崩溃的根因分析，如果有东西能在20分钟内帮你搞定，然后告诉你发生了什么，他们就可以用那段时间去优化引擎，或者帮助内容创作者等等。我觉得这要因地制宜，有些地方它没用，有些地方则有用。」

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : The whole space is moving so fast. We recognized early on that Epic should just broadly enable everybody to use the tools they prefer and be able to plug them into Unreal Engine in any way they want. We didn't go out to build like the Unreal Engine coding model, rather, we built an MCP server so that people could bring Claude code, Gemini, or whatever tool they prefer and connect it. Every week or two, there are going to be new capabilities coming out with lots of different companies competing, and we want to be able to support them all and put each game developer in charge of how they want to integrate AI tools into their pipelines to get maximum usefulness out of it and figure out what really gets acceleration maximized.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney：「整个领域发展太快了。我们早就认识到，Epic应该广泛地让每个人使用自己喜欢的工具，并以他们想要的任何方式接入Unreal Engine。我们没有去构建Unreal Engine的编程模型，而是构建了一个MCP服务器，让人们可以接入Claude代码、Gemini或任何他们偏好的工具，并连接起来。每一两周，各大公司竞争会带来新的能力，我们希望能支持所有这些，让每个游戏开发者自己决定如何将AI工具整合到流水线中，以获取最大效用，并找出真正能实现最大加速的东西。」

paragraphs.en: IGN: There will always be concerns about artistic intent. We saw the demo of how you can build a virtual world foundationally with MCP and Claude coding. There are a lot of people who are on no-AI policies, and these kinds of things get marked on Steam pages as using generative AI. So, when it comes to building worlds and building games, I want to get your thoughts on preserving artistic value when it comes to using AI.
paragraphs.cn: IGN：总会有人担忧艺术意图。我们看到了如何用MCP和Claude编码从基础上构建虚拟世界的演示。有很多人执行无AI政策，这些东西在Steam页面上会被标记为使用了生成式AI。所以，在构建世界和游戏时，我想听听你们对于使用AI时如何保持艺术价值的看法。

paragraphs.en: Wassmer : Sure, we're building all the pipelines in Unreal to maximally preserve artistic intent. We gave the demos and you can see every step of the way. Our intention is, whatever gets built is a real Unreal scene that people can tweak and make exactly the way they wanted it to be rather than just typing into the prompt and trying a million times to get whatever pops out right. It's really meant to help people explore creatively more quickly, then settle in on the details of what they actually want, and then do the tweaks they need by hand right in the engine. So for us, it's human control all the way through the pipeline.
paragraphs.cn: Wassmer：当然，我们在虚幻引擎中构建所有流程，以最大限度地保留艺术意图。我们做了演示，你可以看到过程中的每一步。我们的目的是，构建出来的任何内容都是一个真实的虚幻引擎场景，人们可以对其进行调整，完全按照自己的意愿来实现，而不是仅仅在提示框里输入文字，然后尝试无数次以求得到正确的结果。这旨在帮助人们更快地进行创意探索，然后确定他们真正想要的细节，最后直接在引擎中手动进行所需的调整。所以对我们来说，从始至终都由人掌控整个流程。

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : Yeah, exactly that. You know, gaming has always been driven by great games built by great development teams and that will continue to be the case. Every generation has had its stereotypical low quality games, from just plain old bad games to asset flips and now we’ll have AI slop. But in the hands of awesome professional creators and serious indies building a game, these tools are just an accelerant. And just as the industry moved from pixel art to Photoshop and then from 2D to 3D, these are just going to be ways to make content more efficiently and avoiding the drudgery of handwiring a giant blueprint and debugging really complicated problems in a program.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney：是的，正是如此。你知道，游戏行业一直由出色的开发团队打造的优秀作品推动，未来也依然如此。每一代都有其典型的低质量游戏，从纯粹的老式烂游戏到素材翻新，现在又会有AI烂作。但在优秀的专业创作者和认真的独立开发者手中，这些工具只是加速器。正如行业从像素画过渡到Photoshop，又从2D过渡到3D，这些工具只会使内容制作更高效，避免手工编写庞大蓝图的繁琐，以及调试程序中极其复杂的问题。

paragraphs.en: IGN: I also want to get your perspective on the physical cost of using AI when it comes to data centers and energy usage, and your thoughts on the impact of AI outside of artistic intent. Is that something that is at the top of your mind or are you looking at this as just enabling the tools in Unreal? Is that something that you account for?
paragraphs.cn: IGN：我还想听听你们对使用AI的物理成本的看法，涉及到数据中心和能源消耗，以及你们对AI在艺术意图之外的影响有何想法。这是你们首要考虑的问题吗，还是说你们只是将其视为在虚幻引擎中提供工具？你们会考虑这一点吗？

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : I think that's for the model providers to sort out. Some of them are operating at a loss such that there's actually a higher cost than they're charging to try to grow their businesses. Then there are trade-offs between local AI and server-based hosted AI. I think the whole industry is going to have to sort that out.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney：我认为这需要模型提供商去解决。他们中有些正在亏损运营，实际成本高于他们为拓展业务而收取的费用。此外，本地AI和基于服务器的托管AI之间也需要权衡。我认为整个行业都必须解决这个问题。

paragraphs.en: With our part, Epic doesn't have billions of dollars to invest in building a bleeding edge model to compete with these things. What we can do is build an awesome MCP interface so that every one of them can talk to our engine and so on. I think developers and the market are going to ultimately sort that out, and I think there’s plenty of incentive for everybody to do these things efficiently. Cost is going to be formidable when you're using a bleeding edge model at its maximum capability. And optimizing that is going to be a first-class objective for everybody.
paragraphs.cn: 就我们而言，Epic没有数十亿美元去投资构建一个尖端模型来与这些公司竞争。我们能做的是构建一个出色的MCP接口，让所有这些模型都能与我们的引擎通信等等。我认为开发者和市场最终会解决这个问题，而且大家都有足够的动力去高效地完成这些工作。当你以最大能力使用尖端模型时，成本会很高。而优化成本将是所有人的首要目标。

paragraphs.en: IGN: You’ve been in the business for a long time, and along with the unprecedented times with hardware cost, it’s also unprecedented times with AAA studios more or less collapsing. We've seen big name developers either cutting significantly or being shut down by parent companies. Being part of game development for so many years, I want to get your thoughts on the contraction of a lot of these bigger studios and where you think things are going wrong. What lessons do you think need to be learned from all this?
paragraphs.cn: IGN：你在这个行业已经很久了，在硬件成本前所未有的时代，同时3A级工作室也在或多或少地崩溃。我们看到许多知名开发商要么大幅裁员，要么被母公司关闭。你从事游戏开发这么多年，我想听听你对很多大型工作室收缩的看法，以及你认为哪里出了问题。你觉得需要从中吸取哪些教训？

paragraphs.en: Sweeney : I guess I've been around the longest. But you can't say that this is the first time the industry has gone through a shakeup because there have been many before. I was a young gamer in the 1980s when the Atari crash happened, and the transition from 2D gaming to 3D when suddenly a lot of the games at the time that were being developed ended up not finding a market. And then BitTorrent hit the industry pretty hard, too. There was a time when the industry rumor was that Crysis had sold 100,000 copies and 10 million people had played it. And the solution to that was multifaceted.
paragraphs.cn: Sweeney：我想我是在这行干得最久的了。但你不能说这是行业头一次经历震荡，因为以前已经发生过很多次。我年轻时在 1980 年代玩着游戏，经历了雅达利大崩溃，还有从 2D 游戏到 3D 游戏的转型，当时一大批正在开发的游戏突然就找不到市场了。后来 BitTorrent 也对行业冲击不小。曾有一段时期，业内传闻《孤岛危机》只卖出了 10 万份，却有 1000 万人玩过它。应对这种局面的办法是多方面的。

paragraphs.en: Every technology generation, whether it's seven or ten years – changes accumulate so much that the way people build games and play games changes. The answer can't be that every generation we just spend exponentially more on game development because the market doesn't always support that. And I think we've seen some very specific problems with specific games. Sometimes a really big budget game ships that wasn't very good and didn't sell. But much more often as we've seen with a bunch of the big multiplayer games – it could be a good game – the market dynamics prevented players from coming in simultaneously with enough scale to make it viable.
paragraphs.cn: 每一代技术，不管是七年还是十年，累积的变化都大到足以改变人们开发游戏和玩游戏的方式。但答案不能是每进入一代，我们就在游戏开发上投入指数级增长的资金，因为市场并不总是支撑得住。我觉得我们已经看到一些特定游戏出现了很具体的问题。有时一部超大预算的作品推出后质量不佳，销量也不好。但更常见的情况，正如我们在许多大型多人游戏中所见——游戏本身或许不错——但市场动态导致玩家无法在同一时间以足够大的规模涌入，使其无法立足。

paragraphs.en: We should set aside flukes where a game didn't meet expectations of gamers and just look at more of the structural changes. And those are really appreciable, the fact that games are becoming increasingly multiplayer. And not just multiplayer but social, where you're getting together with your friends and then you're deciding what to do, what to play, and how to play it. This trend of the gaming economy shifting – some people like it and some don't – is going more and more towards buying things in games rather than buying games. In-game economy is driving gaming, especially at scale and especially over long durations as you see with these long-lasting multiplayer games.
paragraphs.cn: 我们应该把那些未能满足玩家期待的游戏案例当成偶发事件放在一边，更多地去审视结构性变化。这些变化确实相当显著，比如游戏正变得越来越强调多人模式。而且不仅是多人模式，更是社交化——你和朋友们聚在一起，一同决定要做什么、玩什么、怎么玩。游戏经济转型的趋势——有些人喜欢，有些人不喜欢——正越来越转向在游戏内购买内容，而非直接购买游戏。游戏内经济正在驱动整个行业，特别是在大规模运营和长线运营中，就像那些长期运营的多人游戏所展现的那样。

paragraphs.en: You have much more of a winner-takes-all phenomenon where it's really hard for any new entrant to compete with an established game, even if it is incrementally better. After years of established games continually reinvesting and making the game better, it's hard for a new entrant to compete with a game that's had many years of development and potentially billions of dollars of development investment going into it over time with a small team building a small game.
paragraphs.cn: 如今「赢家通吃」的现象更加明显，任何新入场者都极难与一款已经站稳脚跟的游戏竞争，哪怕新游戏在某个方面略胜一筹。那些成熟游戏经过多年持续投入、不断优化之后，一款已经开发多年、累计投入可能高达数十亿美元的作品，单靠一个小团队做一款小游戏就想去与它抗衡，难度可想而知。

paragraphs.en: Those are the huge challenges and those are generational, different challenges than you've seen in the past. Each of them was kind of an isolated problem that had a solution [before], and here the answer has got to be pretty broad change in the way that everybody goes about developing games. We've got to develop better games more consistently and we've got to develop them a lot more efficiently.
paragraphs.cn: 这些是巨大的挑战，而且是代际层面的挑战，与过去不同。过去的每一个问题大体都是孤立的，并有对应的解法。而如今，答案必须是整个行业在开发游戏的方式上做出相当广泛的改变。我们必须更稳定地做出更好的游戏，而且开发效率必须大幅提升。
